title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Redirects, Parser Limits, and Output Injection · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in untrusted-input security boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Allowlist schemes and destinations, reject credentials and dangerous address classes, constrain ports, and separate user input from fetch authority.
Resolve and classify every connection destination at the correct time, bind policy to the connected peer where possible, and recheck changes.
Apply full destination, credential, DNS, port, method, and budget policy on every redirect and browser subrequest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug site-quality team handles case 5764 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate SSRF destination policy, DNS and redirect checks, parser limits, and output injection defense; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-output-injection-defense-1201sa
# Competency: scraper-output-injection-defense.
# Case 4161: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_output_injection_defense_1201sa(candidate_destinations: int = 24, admitted_destinations: int = 14, rejected_destinations: int = 10, redirect_rechecks: int = 6, injection_findings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1201sa = "1201sa-as1021-4161"
    assert evidence_variant_1201sa
    evidence_axes_1201sa = (
        "authorization1201sa",
        "url4161",
        "responseas1021",
        "tree1201sa4161",
        "frontier41611201sa",
        "reportas10214161",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1201sa)) == 6
    if candidate_destinations < 1 or admitted_destinations < 0 or rejected_destinations < 0:
        return False, candidate_destinations, "security-count"
    if admitted_destinations + rejected_destinations != candidate_destinations:
        return False, admitted_destinations, "destination-reconciliation"
    if redirect_rechecks < 1:
        return False, redirect_rechecks, "redirect-revalidation"
    if injection_findings > 0:
        return False, injection_findings, "output-injection"
    assert admitted_destinations + rejected_destinations == candidate_destinations and injection_findings == 0
    return True, rejected_destinations, "secured-crawl-boundary"
